claim_results.claim: MacArthur and the Equation Mapping the Calvinistâ€“Arminian war onto the math, and resolving it.
One equation, two terms, both real.
The equation doesn't replace the preaching.
It shows why the preaching was more precise than anyone realized.
When MacArthur says dead in sin , that maps to a specific value in a specific equation.
The equation doesn't replace his preaching.
It shows why his preaching is more precise than he may realize.
If God decided everything in advance and you had no real say, then every altar call is theater, every prayer of repentance is scripted, and whosoever will is a lie.
Not reluctantly, not under coercion, but freely — because God changes their nature first.
MacArthur's analogy: a sheep follows the shepherd because it's a sheep.
He calls the tension an antinomy — two truths that appear to contradict but don't, because the reconciliation lies beyond our finite comprehension.
And both are doing it because they believe the alternative is contradiction.
If God really chose the elect before time, then whoever believes can't mean what it seems to mean.
If whoever believes really means whoever, then chose before the foundation of the world can't mean what it seems to mean.
It requires a specific kind of mathematics that wasn't available until the 20th century.
You're not making a choice because you don't have the capacity to make one.
It must, because the dead cannot initiate their own resurrection.
Not because you earned the opportunity (God provided that) but because within the open channel, your response is real.
But what if God has harmonized them — in one equation, with both terms present, both terms necessary, and neither alone sufficient?
The Equation For readers who want the formal machinery, here it is.
The equation reduces to pure decay: dC/dt = −S · C .
This is MacArthur's "dead in trespasses and sins." The math proves what he preaches: the first move must come from outside.
The growth term — O · G(1−C) — requires both God's input AND human openness.
MacArthur's Specific Claims, Mapped For those who want to see how precisely MacArthur's language maps to the equation:
Once you have the equation, the antinomy dissolves.

The grammatical evidence is striking: Romans 9:22–23 uses a passive verb for "vessels of wrath fitted for destruction" but an active verb for "vessels of mercy, whom He prepared beforehand for glory." The equation maps this cleanly.
At s = −1 with the coupling function at zero, the equation is just dC/dt = −S · C .
Fitted for destruction by its own trajectory. — The Asymmetry Is Not Arbitrary Salvation requires external input.
Destruction requires only the absence of that input.
Romans 9 got the grammar right because the grammar reflects the actual dynamics.

What Augustine Already Knew Twelve centuries before this equation was written, Augustine mapped four states of the human will that correspond precisely to specific values: Augustine's State Latin Phrase Equation Value Description Before the Fall posse peccare, posse non peccare Channel open Able to sin, able not to sin.
The person's will and God's will produce identical output — not because the person was overwritten but because they're fully transparent to grace.
The equation cannot answer this either.
The initial conditions — who receives the effectual call — are set by a Person with reasons the equation doesn't access.
The equation dissolves the mechanism of the contradiction.
Some questions sit outside the equation, inside the character of God.

What This Means Philippians 2:12–13 — the verse MacArthur preaches constantly — states the entire equation in one sentence: "Work out your salvation with fear and trembling, for it is God who works in you, both to will and to work for His good pleasure." "Work out your salvation" = keep the channel open.
"With fear and trembling" = because entropy is always active, always pulling downward, and complacency closes the channel.
Any claim that turns a structural resemblance into a proven mechanism belongs here until the math, experiment, or textual chain can carry the weight.
Possible overstatement: language that sounds final should be read as a working model unless the article explicitly proves it.

Phase 1 is monergistic â€” God's grace $G$ acts on a closed system to lift the coupling function $\alpha(s) = (1+s)/2$ from zero, because at $s = -1$ the equation reduces to pure decay $dC/dt = -S \cdot C$ and the system cannot reverse itself.
Rigor & Kill Conditions Every claim is held to explicit falsification standards.
Every equation that appears in the paper, collected here as a reference index.
The article's central equation; both Calvinism and Arminianism are claimed to map onto its two phases.

Augustine's Four States — in Equation Form $$\text{Pre-Fall: open} \;\to\; \text{Post-Fall: } s=-1,\,\alpha=0 \;\to\; \text{Under Grace: } 0<s<+1 \;\to\; \text{In Glory: } s=+1,\,\alpha=1$$ Augustine: posse peccare, posse non peccare (pre-Fall) → non posse non peccare (post-Fall) → posse non peccare (under grace) → non posse peccare (in glory).
Plain English: Twelve centuries before this equation, Augustine had mapped the four states of the human will.

Passive Reprobation (Romans 9:22–23) $$\text{Election: God acts on}\; s \;\Rightarrow\; \text{active verb} \quad\big|\quad \text{Reprobation: } \frac{dC}{dt} = -S \cdot C \;\Rightarrow\; \text{passive verb}$$ Salvation requires external input.
Destruction requires only its absence.
The state variable in the article's coherence equation; what salvation grows and sin decays.
Always active post-Fall, always pulling C downward in the equation's decay term S·C.
Framework note: Sin (theological frame) and Entropy (physical frame) name the same dynamical quantity at the master-equation level.
The article's claim: what an antinomy looks like in single-phase language is a clean two-phase process when you have the equation.
The equation describes the dynamics of how the transition works; it does not access the reasons God elects some and not others.
The article rejects this in line with MacArthur: at s = −1 with α = 0, the equation is pure decay — God does not push the unelected; their own dynamics carry them downward.
The asymmetry the equation exposes: salvation requires external input (active verb in Romans 9), destruction requires only the absence of that input (passive verb).
In equation terms: G strong enough that the −1 → 0 transition completes.
Not because the will is overridden but because at s = −1 there is nothing actively resisting — only nothing actively receiving.
Galatians 2:20: “not I but Christ.” The article's claim: this is gained freedom, not lost — freedom from the freedom-to-decay.
MacArthur's image: a sheep follows the shepherd because it is a sheep, not because it is coerced.
In equation terms, the question of who receives the effectual call.
The article admits the equation does not access this; it is set by a Person with reasons outside the dynamics.
The article's load-bearing claim: with two phases explicit, the proof texts from each camp slot into their proper phase without reinterpretation.

Philippians 2:12–13 The article's culminating proof text: “work out your salvation with fear and trembling, for it is God who works in you.” Read as the entire equation in one sentence — both terms, both phases, both real.

Logos Field ($\chi$) The pervasive substrate of coherent reality — the framework's $\chi$ field; the Master-Equation field whose value is total system coherence. (Earlier drafts denoted this with $\Lambda$; that symbol is now reserved for the cosmological constant.) Field ≠ coupling : this entry locks the substrate sense.
